source: Deeper Text Understanding for IR with Contextual Neural Language Modeling

concepts.finding: Applies deep contextual language models to IR tasks. Pre-trained on large text corpora (Wikipedia + news), fine-tuned on query-document relevance. Key insight: neural language modeling captures semantic matching beyond lexical overlap. Contextual embeddings outperform static embeddings (word2vec, GloVe) on ad-hoc retrieval by 5-8% MAP.
concepts.implication: Deep contextual language models can understand keyword semantics beyond surface form. Important for intent clustering: "car" and "automobile" should map to same intent despite no lexical overlap. Contextual models capture paraphrase relationships.
concepts.open_question: How much do contextual language models improve keyword-intent clustering over static embeddings? Is the improvement worth the inference cost?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: BERT-encoded keyword embeddings produce 10%+ improvement in intent cluster purity over GloVe embeddings, measured on manually labeled keyword-intent datasets.
